world.title: Left Atrial Appendage Closure Versus Oral Anticoagulation for Stroke Prevention in Non-Valvular Atrial Fibrillation: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials - Cureus
world.summary: Left atrial appendage closure (LAAC) has emerged as a non-pharmacological alternative to oral anticoagulation for stroke prevention in atrial fibrillation (AF). While early randomized trials established non-inferiority of LAAC versus warfarin, the comparative effectiveness of LAAC against contemporary direct oral anticoagulants (DOACs) remains less well characterized. We conducted a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (RCTs) comparing percutaneous LAAC with oral anticoagulation-either warfarin or DOACs-in adults with non-valvular AF.

world.title: GLP-1s: US expands health coverage to include elderly and disabled people, making millions more eligible - The BMJ
world.summary: Millions more Americans will now have access to GLP-1 drugs for weight loss under a new programme launched by the US government. The US administration approved the use of Zepbound/Mounjaro (tirzepatide), Foundayo (orforglipron), and Wegovy (semaglutide) for obesity under a temporary health insurance programme for US seniors and people with long term disabilities. The programme will run until the end of 2027 and will help some of the 40.3% of US adults with obesity (around 112.4 million people) to get access to these drugs.

world.title: Retatrutide fact check: Has a man died after taking the unapproved weight loss jab? - The BMJ
world.summary: The death of a man taking the unapproved next generation GLP-1 weight loss drug retatrutide made headlines in the national press last week. However, the situation may be less clear cut than the articles suggest. The reports were based on data recorded on the UK drug regulator’s adverse events reporting system, the Yellow Card scheme.

world.title: Natural birth ideology and the unavoidable challenge of avoidable harms - The BMJ
world.summary: The startling safety data from two new reviews on maternity services might be expected to trigger immediate measures for improvement. However, the likelihood is that nothing will change quickly. The reasons for this dispiriting conclusion include the simple fact that cultural change, which seems to be at the heart of the maternity services crisis, is hard to achieve in a hurry.

world.title: Amos maternity review methods compounded harm for families - The BMJ
world.summary: Future work to improve maternity care must be trauma informed and listen to the needs of families, writes Jenny Ardley Our maternity system is in crisis. A week after the Ockenden review of maternity services at Nottingham University Hospitals NHS Trust was published,1 the Amos Independent National Maternity and Neonatal Investigation highlighted overlapping, wide scale failures of care and preventable maternal and infant harm.2 Tragically, for me and my family this is not a revelation, it is our lived experience. I was a senior lecturer in criminology when the birth trauma that I experienced in Nottingham caused such severe physical damage and post-traumatic stress disorder, that I lost my working memory and was forced to give up the career I loved.3 After an emergency caesarean section, I suffered through four hours of surgery while fully conscious.

world.title: Community first-responders save lives on Bangladesh’s roads - World Health Organization (WHO)
world.summary: A pilot project in Bangladesh has shown that trained community volunteers can mean the difference between life and death in a road collision. A delay in emergency care by minutes can determine a victim’s fate, and the initiative in Bangladesh ensured that all crash victims received first aid in just five to eight minutes.Based on the number and severity of recent road crashes, a 10 km stretch of a busy national highway was selected for the pilot phase in 2023, with a further 30 km added in 2025. With WHO support, the Bangladesh Directorate General of Health Services piloted the community first responder project which includes a 24-hour emergency hotline, a call centre, and 120 trained volunteer first responders.The volunteers were made up of residents living close to the highway, including shopkeepers, drivers, homemakers and teachers who demonstrated a clear determination to save lives.

world.summary: ### Były sekretarz zdrowia stara się zostać premierem Były brytyjski sekretarz zdrowia Andy Burnham potwierdził swoją długo oczekiwaną kandydaturę na kolejnego premiera po rezygnacji Keira Starmera. Wspierany poparciem Wesa Streetinga – który był typowany jako kandydat Burnhama na najwyższe stanowisko – ustępujący burmistrz Manchesteru jest zdecydowanym faworytem na stanowisko lidera laburzystów po tym, jak zapewnił sobie powrót do parlamentu dzięki zwycięstwu w wyborach uzupełniających w Makerfield. Jeśli się powiedzie, Burnham, który w latach 2009–2010 był sekretarzem zdrowia Gordona Browna, będzie pierwszym byłym sekretarzem zdrowia, który zostanie premierem Wielkiej Brytanii.

world.title: Zintegrowane i systematyczne podejście do genomiki w celu poprawy diagnostyki rzadkich i niezdiagnozowanych chorób: wyniki projektu RareBoost - Natura

W ramach projektu RareBoost wdrożyliśmy zintegrowaną, etapową strategię genomiczną w kohorcie 134 pacjentów ze 120 rodzin z wcześniej niejednoznacznymi wynikami badań genetycznych. Nasza strategia łączyła systematyczne sekwencjonowanie egzomu z późniejszym sekwencjonowaniem genomu, gdy jest to wskazane, a następnie ukierunkowanym sekwencjonowaniem RNA i uzupełniona kompleksowym fenotypowaniem i dalszą analizą segregacji.

Its effects are mediated by two main pharmacological pathways: (1) sympathetic overdrive due to inhibition of norepinephrine reuptake, leading to hypertension, tachycardia, vasospasm, and thrombosis; and (2) sodium and other ion channel blockade, which provokes arrhythmias and impairs contractility.

These barriers may include legal and policy restrictions, stigma, limited provider capacity, financial and geographic constraints, lack of confidentiality and inadequate access to evidence-based information.

It is the most comprehensive assessment ever produced of where the world stands in preventing and controlling cancer. Beyond measuring disease burden and evaluating the performance of health systems, the report places people and their lived experiences at the centre of the global cancer agenda.

epidemiology.title: Zarządzanie bezpieczeństwem ruchu drogowego w Maroku może wyciągnąć wnioski – Światowa Organizacja Zdrowia (WHO)
epidemiology.summary: W 2025 r. Królestwo Maroka było gospodarzem czwartej światowej konferencji ministerialnej na temat bezpieczeństwa ruchu drogowego, podczas której przywódcy zatwierdzili nową deklarację mającą na celu zmniejszenie liczby ofiar śmiertelnych i obrażeń na drogach. Rola Maroka jako organizatora odzwierciedla pozycję tego kraju jako jednego z najbardziej zaangażowanych podmiotów w dziedzinie bezpieczeństwa drogowego w regionie Afryki. W ostatnich latach Maroko zbudowało nową, kluczową architekturę instytucjonalną i systemy danych oraz wykazało wolę przełożenia strategii na działania. Liczba ofiar śmiertelnych wypadków drogowych w kraju obecnie spada. W miarę jak rządy zbierają się na spotkaniu wysokiego szczebla ONZ w sprawie poprawy światowego bezpieczeństwa ruchu drogowego w lipcu 2026 r., doświadczenie Maroka zapewnia wiedzę, z której mogą korzystać kraje na każdym poziomie rozwoju.

epidemiology.title: Community first-responders save lives on Bangladesh’s roads - World Health Organization (WHO)
epidemiology.summary: A pilot project in Bangladesh has shown that trained community volunteers can mean the difference between life and death in a road collision. A delay in emergency care by minutes can determine a victim’s fate, and the initiative in Bangladesh ensured that all crash victims received first aid in just five to eight minutes.Based on the number and severity of recent road crashes, a 10 km stretch of a busy national highway was selected for the pilot phase in 2023, with a further 30 km added in 2025. With WHO support, the Bangladesh Directorate General of Health Services piloted the community first responder project which includes a 24-hour emergency hotline, a call centre, and 120 trained volunteer first responders.The volunteers were made up of residents living close to the highway, including shopkeepers, drivers, homemakers and teachers who demonstrated a clear determination to save lives.

clinical_trials.summary: A co powiesz na sprawę przeciwko abolicji, a także sprawę o odpowiedzialność? Była dyrektor Narodowego Instytutu Zdrowia (NIH) i obecna prezes Narodowej Akademii Medycznej Monica Bertagnolli włączyła się w rosnącą debatę na temat przyszłości NIH, odpowiadając bezpośrednio na niedawną opinię Scotta Atlasa w „Washington Post” wzywającą do rozwiązania agencji. Bertagnolli argumentuje, że likwidacja NIH znacząco osłabiłaby amerykański ekosystem badań biomedycznych, wskazując na dziesięciolecia odkryć finansowanych przez podatników, które pomogły w stworzeniu naukowych podstaw pod terapie przeciwnowotworowe, technologie mRNA, leki GLP-1, genomikę i wiele innych osiągnięć medycznych.

W badaniu prowadzonym przez dr Judith M. Ford, profesor psychiatrii na Uniwersytecie Kalifornijskim w San Francisco (UCSF) i Weill Institute for Neurosciences, wzięło udział 58 uczestników, z czego 47 ukończyło miesięczne randomizowane porównanie, a 25 kontynuowało badanie o cztery miesiące.

clinical_trials.title: Are Lipid Nanoparticles Really Inert? New Review Raises a Serious Mechanistic Question - trialsitenews.com
clinical_trials.summary: Falko Seger, L. Maria Gutschi, and Stephanie Seneff published a hypothesis-driven review in Acta Pharmaceutica Sinica B titled “Lipid nanoparticles as active biointerfaces: From membrane interaction to systemic dysregulation.” The authors ask whether lipid nanoparticles (LNPs), including those used in mRNA vaccines and RNA therapeutics, may act as biologically active membrane-interacting systems rather than passive delivery shells. Their proposed model—lipid-nanoparticle-driven membrane dysfunction (L-DMD)—argues that ionizable lipids may perturb phosphoinositide signaling, endosomal trafficking, inflammatory pathways, and metabolic regulation.

clinical_trials.summary: Badacze z Albert Einstein College of Medicine, LonGenity Study, Stony Brook Medicine i współpracownicy podają, że dorośli w wieku 80 lat i starsi, sklasyfikowani jako „super poruszający się”, czyli osoby charakteryzujące się wyjątkowo dużą szybkością chodu jak na swój wiek i płeć, byli narażeni na prawie 50% mniejsze ryzyko wystąpienia zaburzeń poznawczych w porównaniu z ich rówieśnikami. W retrospektywnej analizie opublikowanej w czasopiśmie Neurology wzięło udział prawie 4000 starszych osób dorosłych z międzynarodowych kohort starzenia się, w tym z międzynarodowej sieci studiów Health and Retirement Study (HRS-INS), LonGenity Study oraz Rush Memory and Aging Project (RUSH MAP). Do głównych badaczy należeli dr.

clinical_trials.title: Catholic Health and Columbia Expand Cancer Clinical Trials Across Long Island—Access Improves, But Enrollment Questions Remain - trialsitenews.com
clinical_trials.summary: Catholic Health has partnered with the Herbert Irving Comprehensive Cancer Center (HICCC) at NewYork-Presbyterian/Columbia University Irving Medical Center to expand access to oncology clinical trials for patients across Long Island. The collaboration immediately launches approximately 20 cancer clinical trials, with additional studies planned, bringing investigational therapies closer to patients receiving care in community oncology practices. The initiative allows eligible patients treated at Catholic Health Cancer Institute locations to participate in studies evaluating precision medicine, targeted therapies, immunotherapies, and novel cancer treatment strategies while remaining under the care of their local oncology teams.

clinical_trials.title: Historyczny strajk Brighama i pielęgniarki kobiecej testuje napięcia między medycyną akademicką a korporacyjną opieką zdrowotną
clinical_trials.summary: Około 4000 dyplomowanych pielęgniarek w Brigham and Women's Hospital odeszło w środę z pracy w wyniku, co Stowarzyszenie Pielęgniarek Massachusetts (MNA) określiło jako największy strajk pielęgniarek i pracowników służby zdrowia w historii Massachusetts. Chociaż strajk zaplanowany jest na jeden dzień, generał Mass Brigham (MGB) przedłużył przerwę w pracy do pięciu dni po zatrudnieniu pielęgniarek zastępczych do utrzymania działalności szpitala. Spór koncentruje się na płacach, świadczeniach, poziomie zatrudnienia, warunkach w miejscu pracy i tym, co wiele pielęgniarek określa jako coraz bardziej korporacyjną kulturę zarządzania po integracji Brighama z systemem opieki zdrowotnej MGB.

clinical_trials.title: Can Probiotics Help Protect the Aging Brain? Major Review Highlights Promise of the Gut-Brain Axis—But Clinical Evidence Remains Mixed - trialsitenews.com
clinical_trials.summary: Lead author Sneha Dutta of the All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), Bhubaneswar, together with collaborators including researchers from Purdue University, has published a comprehensive review in Probiotics and Antimicrobial Proteins examining whether probiotics can help preserve cognitive function by targeting the microbiota-gut-brain axis during aging. Rather than presenting new experimental findings, the review synthesizes more than 500 published studies exploring how gut microbes influence brain health, inflammation, and cognition. The authors conclude that selected probiotic strains—particularly Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium—show promise for supporting cognitive health through multiple biological pathways.

clinical_trials.title: The Tiny Microbe Behind Medicine's Next Frontier? Analysis Reveals Surge in Bifidobacteria Clinical Trials - trialsitenews.com
clinical_trials.summary: What was once viewed primarily as a digestive probiotic is steadily emerging as a serious area of biomedical investigation. A TrialSite News analysis of 36 registered studies on ClinicalTrials.gov finds that Bifidobacterium is increasingly being studied not only for gastrointestinal disorders but also for cognitive decline, cancer immunotherapy, metabolic disease, liver disease, mental health, healthy aging, surgical recovery, and pediatric eye disease. While definitive clinical evidence remains limited across many indications, the expanding research pipeline suggests microbiome therapeutics are entering a new era of precision medicine built on biomarkers, targeted microbial strains, and personalized interventions.

clinical_trials.title: Badania kliniczne nad autyzmem wkraczają w nową erę: ClinicalTrials.gov ujawnia trzy trendy zmieniające badania nad autyzmem - trialssitenews.com
clinical_trials.summary: Badania nad zaburzeniami ze spektrum autyzmu (ASD) przechodzą ważną transformację. Analiza TrialSite News obejmująca 58 interwencyjnych badań nad autyzmem zarejestrowanych na ClinicalTrials.gov ujawnia dziedzinę wykraczającą poza szerokie leczenie objawów w stronę coraz bardziej ukierunkowanych, zindywidualizowanych interwencji. Zamiast szukać jednej metody leczenia autyzmu, badacze opracowują terapie ukierunkowane na określone szlaki biologiczne, domeny behawioralne i podgrupy pacjentów.

clinical_trials.title: KFF Poll Suggests Americans Are Less Polarized on Vaccine Claims Than Public Debate Implies - trialsitenews.com
clinical_trials.summary: Public debates over vaccines often portray America as deeply divided between unwavering believers and committed skeptics. A new Kaiser Family Foundation (KFF) national survey suggests a more complicated reality: only a small fraction of Americans fully embrace what the health institutional venture deems common vaccine myths, while a much larger share remains uncertain and potentially persuadable. The "Malleable Middle" May Matter More Than the Extremes.

clinical_trials.title: One in Five Hospitalized Children Developed Severe Respiratory Disease, Canadian Study Finds - trialsitenews.com
clinical_trials.summary: Researchers led by Dr. Haifa Mtaweh of The Hospital for Sick Children (SickKids), Toronto, report in JAMA Network Open that 21.3% of 2,585 children hospitalized with acute respiratory tract infections (RTIs) developed severe disease requiring advanced respiratory support, extracorporeal therapies, cardiac resuscitation, or resulting in death. The multicenter retrospective cohort study, conducted at two Canadian pediatric hospitals between July 2022 and June 2023, found that children with multiple chronic medical conditions and those requiring interhospital transfer were significantly more likely to experience severe illness.

clinical_trials.title: COVID-19 Pandemic Period Associated with an Estimated 68,000 Excess Maternal Deaths Worldwide, Global Modeling Study Suggests - trialsitenews.com
clinical_trials.summary: Jaimini Hitendra Gajjar of the Georgia State University School of Public Health, together with colleagues from the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine and Georgia State University, reports in a new medRxiv preprint that maternal mortality during the COVID-19 pandemic period (2020–2023) exceeded what would have been expected based on pre-pandemic trends. Using WHO/UN Maternal Mortality Estimation Inter-Agency Group (MMEIG) estimates and a counterfactual forecasting model, the investigators estimated 68,489 excess maternal deaths globally (95% UI 34,706–147,118) and an aggregate excess maternal mortality ratio (MMR) of 10,154. The greatest burdens were concentrated in South-East Asia, the Eastern Mediterranean Region, and Africa.

critical_alerts.summary: To add to an already difficult public health crisis, some healthcare workers in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) have gone on strike, saying they are fighting an Ebola virus in an outbreak with inadequate equipment and low wages, the Associated Press (AP) reports . daily_top5.title: Building research readiness for respiratory pathogen pandemics in South-East Asia through its WHO GISRS Unity Studies regional chapter - World Health Organization (WHO)
daily_top5.summary: Recognizing the importance of timely evidence for informed decision-making and effective emergency response, countries in the WHO South‑East Asia Region (SEAR) have prioritized strengthening their research preparedness capacity. This need became particularly evident during recent outbreaks, when gaps in the rapid generation and use of standardised data limited early response actions.

daily_top5.title: Morocco expands influenza laboratory and surveillance capacity with PIP support - World Health Organization (WHO)
daily_top5.summary: Over more than a decade, Morocco has strengthened its influenza laboratory and surveillance capacities through sustained support from the Pandemic Influenza Preparedness (PIP) Framework. These investments have delivered measurable improvements in diagnostic coverage, timely and complete data generation, and reporting, supporting more effective detection of and response to influenza and other respiratory threats.

daily_top5.title: Domestic external quality assessment programme (EQA) strengthens Ukraine’s respiratory-virus diagnostic readiness - World Health Organization (WHO)
daily_top5.summary: Reliable laboratory testing is central to pandemic preparedness. It allows countries to detect influenza and other priority respiratory viruses early; report results consistently and share quality data that guide timely public health action.
